This stage is cross-cutting prompt infrastructure: it defines the raw text assets and typed context fragments that later prompt-assembly code stitches into model input during startup, normal turns, mode switches, and compatibility replay of older sessions. The prompt-asset files provide the canonical embedded instruction text for agent coordination, apply_patch usage, compact summarization, and realtime mode lifecycle messages.

The fragment definitions turn runtime state into stable model-visible blocks. Environment, token-budget, internal-context, user-instructions, shell-command, subagent-notification, approved-command-prefix, network-rule, turn-aborted, model-switch, guardian reminder, and hook/additional-context modules each serialize a specific kind of session fact or guidance with consistent tags, roles, and truncation rules. Realtime start/end fragments wrap the realtime protocol, while the dynamic variant allows custom startup instructions. Skill-related adapters bridge internal skill data into generic contextual fragments: one path formats available skills and individual skill prompts, and another wraps injected skill instructions for inclusion as user-context. Finally, the legacy warning recognizers preserve compatibility by identifying obsolete fragment shapes in stored conversations so newer code can safely interpret or ignore them.
